name: Esquivel-Quintana v. Sessions
question: Should “unlawful sexual intercourse with a minor” as defined by California law be considered the aggravated felony of “sexual abuse of a minor” under the Immigration and Nationality Act and therefore require mandatory removal?
description: A case in which the Court determined that the crime of “unlawful sexual intercourse with a minor” as defined by California law cannot be categorically considered the aggravated felony of “sexual abuse of a minor” for the purpose of mandatory removal under the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA).
